channel.title: Your Company JIRA
channel.description: An XML representation of a search request

item.title: [PROJ-1] [Epic] Multi-tenant Workspace
item.summary: [Epic] Multi-tenant Workspace
item.description:
h2. Goal
Deliver _Multi-tenant Workspace_ for the *Product Platform* area. This epic consolidates the cross-team work needed to reach a coherent, measurable outcome. Today the experience is fragmented across three teams and two codebases, and there is no single owner for the end-to-end user journey. Shipping this as an epic lets us align incentives and track progress against a single definition of done.
h2. Context
Customer research over the last two quarters keeps surfacing the same pain points: setup is confusing, the middle of the flow drops users, and the finish state doesn't feel rewarding. Support tickets tagged with this area have grown roughly 35% quarter-over-quarter, and the NPS for this surface sits 12 points below the product average. Existing workarounds rely on internal scripts that we want to retire.
h2. Success metrics
* Adoption: > 40% of MAU touch the new surface within 60 days of GA * Reliability: zero P1 incidents attributed to this work during rollout * Support: −30% tickets tagged with this area 90 days after GA * Performance: p95 latency on the primary flow stays under 200ms * Satisfaction: CSAT for this surface improves by at least 10 points
h2. Non-goals
* Rewriting the underlying storage layer * Adding unrelated features to the same surface during migration * Mobile parity in phase 1 — tracked under a separate epic
h2. Workstreams
# **Discovery & design** – validated prototypes, research debriefs, spec # **Platform work** – API contract, data model, feature-flag scaffolding # **UX delivery** – redesigned flows, empty/loading/error states, i18n # **Migration** – dual-write, backfill job, cutover runbook, cleanup # **Observability** – dashboards, SLOs, alerting, on-call runbook entry # **Enablement** – internal docs, external API reference, support FAQ
h2. Risks & dependencies
* Platform team's IAM roles refactor must land before migration cutover * Billing team owns one upstream integration that will need a new webhook * Legal review is on the critical path for any PII handling changes * Data team needs to backfill 3 historical tables (scheduled, ~2 weeks)
h2. Timeline (target)
* Week 1–2: discovery, research synthesis, RFC drafting * Week 3–6: build behind feature flag, internal dogfood * Week 7–8: beta at 10% → 25% of MAU with guardrail metrics * Week 9: GA announcement, enablement content published * Week 10+: deprecate legacy code path, delete dead code
Tracked stories and tasks are linked under this epic. Open questions and weekly status updates live in the pinned thread in #epic channel.

item.title: [PROJ-6] Investigate OOM in batch job in checkout
item.summary: Investigate OOM in batch job in checkout
item.description:
h2. Summary
Tracking implementation work for feature flags in the notifications service. This is part of the broader effort to we save costs. The current implementation is a stop-gap from the v5.1 release and needs to be replaced before we onboard the next cohort.
h2. Plan
# Audit current behavior and write down the invariants we want to preserve # Draft a short RFC (RFC-159) covering data model, API contract, rollback path # Implement behind the `rollout_proj-6` feature flag with a kill switch # Cover new code with unit + integration tests; target ≥ 80% branch coverage # Roll out to the beta cohort (~10% of MAU) for 14 days # GA after 14 days of stability and no new P1 incidents
h2. Technical design notes
We considered three approaches:
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Decision || | Extend existing notifications service | fastest to ship, reuses patterns | couples us to legacy code | | | Extract a dedicated service | clean seams, independent scaling | infra cost, more on-call | | | Use a SaaS primitive | least effort | vendor lock-in, PII concerns | rejected |
Default pick: **extend existing notifications service** unless profiling on staging shows we can't hit the 50 req/s target with p95 latency under 200ms.
h2. Risks & mitigations
* **Data migration:** writes double-written during rollout. Backfill job gated by flag. * **On-call load:** new dashboards + runbook entries land in the same PR as the feature. * **Rollback:** feature flag flip restores old path within seconds.
*Estimated effort:* 21 story points. Dependencies: platform team's IAM work.

comments.comment: Pushed a draft PR — would appreciate eyes on the lock acquisition order.

item.title: [PROJ-7] [Bug] Add dark mode to notifications service
item.summary: [Bug] Add dark mode to notifications service
item.description:
h2. Context
We noticed memory leak under load in the rate limiter affecting end user across the production environment. Impact started around 2026-04-10, correlating with the v5.2-hotfix release. At peak we observed roughly 7.1% error rate on the affected endpoints, with users reporting slow response times and occasional 5xx errors. Support ticket volume doubled during the incident window.
h2. Reproduction steps
# Open the rate limiter in a clean session on production # Trigger the flow that calls GET /api/v2/search # Apply moderate load (~500 req/s sustained for 5 minutes) # Observe latency spike and intermittent memory leak under load
h2. Acceptance criteria
* memory leak under load reproduced locally with a deterministic test harness * Root cause identified and captured in a short RFC * Regression test added that fails on main before the fix and passes after * Dashboards in Grafana updated so we'd detect this before customers do * Runbook entry in rate limiter runbook references this ticket
h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at the migration job that runs every night at 02:00 UTC. We should check the latest changes in rate limiter that touched the GET /api/v2/search handler. Felipe Costa already pulled flamegraphs from production; they're attached in the incident channel.
h2. Links
* Incident channel: #inc-proj-7 * Related dashboards: Grafana > rate limiter > Overview * Previous similar issue: see linked tickets
*Estimated effort:* 13 story points. Discussed in the 2026-04-10 sync. Owner: Felipe Costa.

comments.comment: Closing as duplicate of related ticket — same root cause.
Discussed offline. We'll defer this until after the migration.
Verified in staging. Ready for production rollout.

item.title: [PROJ-8] Refactor checkout for I can move faster
item.summary: Refactor checkout for I can move faster
item.description:
h2. Spike goal
Evaluate options for PII redaction in the sync engine and land with a clear recommendation. Current pain: OOM in batch job is surfacing in us-east-1 at ~12.5% rate, and ad-hoc fixes aren't sticking. We need a durable direction before committing engineering time.
h2. Questions to answer
# What's the smallest viable change that would reduce OOM in batch job by at least 50%? # Can we reuse something from the sync engine or do we need new infra? # What's the operational cost (on-call, observability, cost per request)? # Are there open-source projects we should evaluate before building?
h2. Options considered
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Est. effort || | Build in-house | full control, fits our stack | maintenance cost, longer ship time | 5 pts | | Use OSS (OpenTelemetry Collector) | faster to prototype, active community | tuning required, unclear SLAs | 2 pts | | SaaS (Stripe Billing) | least upfront effort | vendor lock-in, PII and compliance | 3 pts | | Do nothing | zero cost | pain persists, risk escalates | n/a |
h2. Validation plan
* Prototype the top option in a throwaway branch * Run load test at 250 req/s for 30 minutes on staging * Collect p50/p95/p99 latency and memory + CPU usage * Share findings in the 2026-04-13 architecture review
h2. Output
* Short ADR (architecture decision record) in `/docs/adr/` * Follow-up tickets with sized estimates * If we decide to build, a tracking Epic
*Time-box:* 3 working days. Owner: the assignee.

comments.comment: Discussed offline. We'll defer this until after the migration.
Reproduced locally on commit abc123. Working on a fix in branch fix/proj-8.
Closing as duplicate of related ticket — same root cause.
Closing as duplicate of related ticket — same root cause.

item.title: [OPS-5] Allow developer to export reports as CSV
item.summary: Allow developer to export reports as CSV
item.description:
h2. Spike goal
Evaluate options for OpenTelemetry tracing in the embedding cache and land with a clear recommendation. Current pain: intermittent 502s is surfacing in staging at ~1.2% rate, and ad-hoc fixes aren't sticking. We need a durable direction before committing engineering time.
h2. Questions to answer
# What's the smallest viable change that would reduce intermittent 502s by at least 50%? # Can we reuse something from the embedding cache or do we need new infra? # What's the operational cost (on-call, observability, cost per request)? # Are there open-source projects we should evaluate before building?
h2. Options considered
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Est. effort || | Build in-house | full control, fits our stack | maintenance cost, longer ship time | 5 pts | | Use OSS (LiteFS) | faster to prototype, active community | tuning required, unclear SLAs | 3 pts | | SaaS (Cloudflare Workers) | least upfront effort | vendor lock-in, PII and compliance | 1 pts | | Do nothing | zero cost | pain persists, risk escalates | n/a |
h2. Validation plan
* Prototype the top option in a throwaway branch * Run load test at 250 req/s for 30 minutes on staging * Collect p50/p95/p99 latency and memory + CPU usage * Share findings in the 2026-04-07 architecture review
h2. Output
* Short ADR (architecture decision record) in `/docs/adr/` * Follow-up tickets with sized estimates * If we decide to build, a tracking Epic
*Time-box:* 3 working days. Owner: the assignee.

comments.comment: Pushed a draft PR — would appreciate eyes on the lock acquisition order.
Tagging Eva Schmidt for review. Looks good apart from the missing test.
Verified in staging. Ready for production rollout.

item.title: [PROJ-9] Fix memory leak under load affecting admin
item.summary: Fix memory leak under load affecting admin
item.description:
h2. Mission
Consolidate how support agent interact with keyboard shortcuts across the checkout. Today the experience is fragmented because this surface has accumulated 3+ years of ad-hoc additions. Our goal is a coherent design with clear contracts, measurable adoption, and a migration path that doesn't leave old integrations stranded.
h2. Success metrics
* Adoption: ≥ 40% of active accounts using the new surface within 60 days of GA * Support load: −30% tickets tagged `checkout` within 90 days * Performance: p95 latency on the hot path ≤ 150ms * Reliability: no new P1 incidents attributed to this work in the first two quarters
h2. Workstreams
# **API & data model** – new contract, versioning, deprecation policy # **UX** – redesign of the primary flow, empty/loading/error states # **Migration** – dual-write, backfill, cutover, cleanup # **Observability** – dashboards, SLOs, on-call runbook # **Enablement** – docs for internal teams + external API consumers
h2. Non-goals
* Rewriting the underlying storage layer * Adding new surface-level features during migration * Mobile parity in phase 1
h2. Timeline (target)

item.title: [OPS-6] Refactor migration scripts for we save costs
item.summary: Refactor migration scripts for we save costs
item.description:
h2. Summary
Tracking implementation work for structured logging in the auth flow. This is part of the broader effort to we reduce support load. The current implementation is a stop-gap from the v5.2-hotfix release and needs to be replaced before we onboard the next cohort.
h2. Plan
# Audit current behavior and write down the invariants we want to preserve # Draft a short RFC (RFC-835) covering data model, API contract, rollback path # Implement behind the `rollout_ops-6` feature flag with a kill switch # Cover new code with unit + integration tests; target ≥ 80% branch coverage # Roll out to the beta cohort (~25% of MAU) for 14 days # GA after 14 days of stability and no new P1 incidents
h2. Technical design notes
We considered three approaches:
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Decision || | Extend existing auth flow | fastest to ship, reuses patterns | couples us to legacy code | | | Extract a dedicated service | clean seams, independent scaling | infra cost, more on-call | | | Use a SaaS primitive | least effort | vendor lock-in, PII concerns | rejected |
Default pick: **extend existing auth flow** unless profiling on staging shows we can't hit the 500 req/s target with p95 latency under 200ms.
h2. Risks & mitigations
* **Data migration:** writes double-written during rollout. Backfill job gated by flag. * **On-call load:** new dashboards + runbook entries land in the same PR as the feature. * **Rollback:** feature flag flip restores old path within seconds.
*Estimated effort:* 3 story points. Dependencies: platform team's IAM work.

comments.comment: This is blocked on the platform team finishing the new IAM roles.

item.title: [OPS-7] Add structured logging to migration scripts
item.summary: Add structured logging to migration scripts
item.description:
h2. Goal
Enable guest user to rotate API keys without downtime, so that we save costs. Today this is painful because users have to work around it by chaining multiple manual steps, which creates support load and makes the product feel inconsistent.
h2. User story
> As a guest user, > I want to rotate API keys without downtime, > so that we save costs.
h2. Acceptance criteria
* The migration scripts exposes a first-class UI for this action * Feature is gated behind a feature flag `rollout_ops-7` rolled out at 5% → 25% → 100% * Empty-state, loading, success and error paths are all covered * Copy reviewed by the product writer and available in English + Polish * Telemetry: at least one success event and one failure event tracked
h2. Out of scope
* Mobile parity (tracked separately) * Bulk operations on more than 100 items * Admin-level permissions for this action
h2. Open questions
# Do we need an audit trail entry for every use of this action? # Should failure notifications go to the user, the admin, or both? # What's the expected rate limit? Current proposal: 50 per user per minute.
h2. Design & references
* Figma: _migration scripts v2 → structured logging_ frame * Linked OKR: _Reduce time-to-value for new accounts_ * Related RFC: RFC-492 (migration scripts structured logging)
*Estimated effort:* 8 story points. Target sprint: the one after kickoff.

item.title: [OPS-12] Fix drift between primary and replica affecting support agent
item.summary: Fix drift between primary and replica affecting support agent
item.description:
h2. Context
We noticed drift between primary and replica in the websocket layer affecting end user across the canary environment. Impact started around 2026-03-24, correlating with the v5.1 release. At peak we observed roughly 12.5% error rate on the affected endpoints, with users reporting slow response times and occasional 5xx errors. Support ticket volume doubled during the incident window.
h2. Reproduction steps
# Open the websocket layer in a clean session on canary # Trigger the flow that calls POST /api/v2/workspaces # Apply moderate load (~500 req/s sustained for 5 minutes) # Observe latency spike and intermittent drift between primary and replica
h2. Acceptance criteria
* drift between primary and replica reproduced locally with a deterministic test harness * Root cause identified and captured in a short RFC * Regression test added that fails on main before the fix and passes after * Dashboards in Grafana updated so we'd detect this before customers do * Runbook entry in websocket layer runbook references this ticket
h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at a lock ordering change landed two weeks ago. We should check the latest changes in websocket layer that touched the POST /api/v2/workspaces handler. Grace Liu already pulled flamegraphs from canary; they're attached in the incident channel.
h2. Links
* Incident channel: #inc-ops-12 * Related dashboards: Grafana > websocket layer > Overview * Previous similar issue: see linked tickets
*Estimated effort:* 8 story points. Discussed in the 2026-03-24 sync. Owner: Grace Liu.

comments.comment: Reproduced locally on commit abc123. Working on a fix in branch fix/ops-12.
Pushed a draft PR — would appreciate eyes on the lock acquisition order.
Discussed offline. We'll defer this until after the migration.

item.description:
h2. Context
We noticed race condition in worker pool in the graph indexer affecting billing manager across the canary environment. Impact started around 2026-04-04, correlating with the v4.12 release. At peak we observed roughly 0.5% error rate on the affected endpoints, with users reporting slow response times and occasional 5xx errors. Support ticket volume doubled during the incident window.
h2. Reproduction steps
# Open the graph indexer in a clean session on canary # Trigger the flow that calls POST /api/v2/workspaces # Apply moderate load (~250 req/s sustained for 5 minutes) # Observe latency spike and intermittent race condition in worker pool
h2. Acceptance criteria
* race condition in worker pool reproduced locally with a deterministic test harness * Root cause identified and captured in a short RFC * Regression test added that fails on main before the fix and passes after * Dashboards in Grafana updated so we'd detect this before customers do * Runbook entry in graph indexer runbook references this ticket
h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at an undocumented assumption about clock monotonicity. We should check the latest changes in graph indexer that touched the POST /api/v2/workspaces handler. Chloé Martin already pulled flamegraphs from canary; they're attached in the incident channel.
h2. Links
* Incident channel: #inc-ops-13 * Related dashboards: Grafana > graph indexer > Overview * Previous similar issue: see linked tickets
*Estimated effort:* 3 story points. Discussed in the 2026-04-04 sync. Owner: Chloé Martin.

item.title: [PROJ-14] Allow support agent to schedule recurring jobs
item.summary: Allow support agent to schedule recurring jobs
item.description:
h2. Summary
Tracking implementation work for E2E test suite in the graph indexer. This is part of the broader effort to users trust the system. The current implementation is a stop-gap from the v5.0-beta release and needs to be replaced before we onboard the next cohort.
h2. Plan
# Audit current behavior and write down the invariants we want to preserve # Draft a short RFC (RFC-294) covering data model, API contract, rollback path # Implement behind the `ff_proj-14` feature flag with a kill switch # Cover new code with unit + integration tests; target ≥ 80% branch coverage # Roll out to the beta cohort (~15% of MAU) for 14 days # GA after 14 days of stability and no new P1 incidents
h2. Technical design notes
We considered three approaches:
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Decision || | Extend existing graph indexer | fastest to ship, reuses patterns | couples us to legacy code | | | Extract a dedicated service | clean seams, independent scaling | infra cost, more on-call | | | Use a SaaS primitive | least effort | vendor lock-in, PII concerns | rejected |
Default pick: **extend existing graph indexer** unless profiling on staging shows we can't hit the 1000 req/s target with p95 latency under 200ms.
h2. Risks & mitigations
* **Data migration:** writes double-written during rollout. Backfill job gated by flag. * **On-call load:** new dashboards + runbook entries land in the same PR as the feature. * **Rollback:** feature flag flip restores old path within seconds.
*Estimated effort:* 3 story points. Dependencies: platform team's IAM work.

# **API & data model** – new contract, versioning, deprecation policy # **UX** – redesign of the primary flow, empty/loading/error states # **Migration** – dual-write, backfill, cutover, cleanup # **Observability** – dashboards, SLOs, on-call runbook # **Enablement** – docs for internal teams + external API consumers
h2. Non-goals
* Rewriting the underlying storage layer * Adding new surface-level features during migration * Mobile parity in phase 1
h2. Timeline (target)
* Week 1–2: discovery + RFC-183 * Week 3–6: build behind `ff_proj-15` flag * Week 7: internal dogfood * Week 8–9: beta at 15% of MAU * Week 10: GA + announcement

item.title: [PROJ-16] As a support agent, I want to filter results by tag so that we meet compliance requirements
item.summary: As a support agent, I want to filter results by tag so that we meet compliance requirements
item.description:
h2. Summary
Tracking implementation work for structured logging in the sync engine. This is part of the broader effort to we save costs. The current implementation is a stop-gap from the v5.0-beta release and needs to be replaced before we onboard the next cohort.
h2. Plan
# Audit current behavior and write down the invariants we want to preserve # Draft a short RFC (RFC-703) covering data model, API contract, rollback path # Implement behind the `experiment_proj-16` feature flag with a kill switch # Cover new code with unit + integration tests; target ≥ 80% branch coverage # Roll out to the beta cohort (~25% of MAU) for 14 days # GA after 14 days of stability and no new P1 incidents
h2. Technical design notes
We considered three approaches:
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Decision || | Extend existing sync engine | fastest to ship, reuses patterns | couples us to legacy code | | | Extract a dedicated service | clean seams, independent scaling | infra cost, more on-call | | | Use a SaaS primitive | least effort | vendor lock-in, PII concerns | rejected |
Default pick: **extend existing sync engine** unless profiling on staging shows we can't hit the 500 req/s target with p95 latency under 200ms.
h2. Risks & mitigations
* **Data migration:** writes double-written during rollout. Backfill job gated by flag. * **On-call load:** new dashboards + runbook entries land in the same PR as the feature. * **Rollback:** feature flag flip restores old path within seconds.
*Estimated effort:* 8 story points. Dependencies: platform team's IAM work.

item.title: [OPS-18] Refactor websocket layer for users trust the system
item.summary: Refactor websocket layer for users trust the system
item.description:
h2. Context
We noticed broken pagination on cold cache in the graph indexer affecting billing manager across the canary environment. Impact started around 2026-03-28, correlating with the v4.13-rc1 release. At peak we observed roughly 12.5% error rate on the affected endpoints, with users reporting slow response times and occasional 5xx errors. Support ticket volume doubled during the incident window.
h2. Reproduction steps
# Open the graph indexer in a clean session on canary # Trigger the flow that calls POST /api/v1/webhooks/dispatch # Apply moderate load (~2500 req/s sustained for 5 minutes) # Observe latency spike and intermittent broken pagination on cold cache
h2. Acceptance criteria
* broken pagination on cold cache reproduced locally with a deterministic test harness * Root cause identified and captured in a short RFC * Regression test added that fails on main before the fix and passes after * Dashboards in Grafana updated so we'd detect this before customers do * Runbook entry in graph indexer runbook references this ticket
h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at a lock ordering change landed two weeks ago. We should check the latest changes in graph indexer that touched the POST /api/v1/webhooks/dispatch handler. Felipe Costa already pulled flamegraphs from canary; they're attached in the incident channel.
h2. Links
* Incident channel: #inc-ops-18 * Related dashboards: Grafana > graph indexer > Overview * Previous similar issue: see linked tickets
*Estimated effort:* 8 story points. Discussed in the 2026-03-28 sync. Owner: Felipe Costa.

comments.comment: Tagging Chloé Martin for review. Looks good apart from the missing test.

item.title: [OPS-19] Allow end user to filter results by tag
item.summary: Allow end user to filter results by tag
item.description:
h2. Spike goal
Evaluate options for API rate-limit headers in the search pipeline and land with a clear recommendation. Current pain: TLS handshake timeouts is surfacing in staging at ~1.2% rate, and ad-hoc fixes aren't sticking. We need a durable direction before committing engineering time.
h2. Questions to answer
# What's the smallest viable change that would reduce TLS handshake timeouts by at least 50%? # Can we reuse something from the search pipeline or do we need new infra? # What's the operational cost (on-call, observability, cost per request)? # Are there open-source projects we should evaluate before building?
h2. Options considered
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Est. effort || | Build in-house | full control, fits our stack | maintenance cost, longer ship time | 21 pts | | Use OSS (OpenTelemetry Collector) | faster to prototype, active community | tuning required, unclear SLAs | 5 pts | | SaaS (Datadog) | least upfront effort | vendor lock-in, PII and compliance | 2 pts | | Do nothing | zero cost | pain persists, risk escalates | n/a |
h2. Validation plan
* Prototype the top option in a throwaway branch * Run load test at 250 req/s for 30 minutes on staging * Collect p50/p95/p99 latency and memory + CPU usage * Share findings in the 2026-03-22 architecture review
h2. Output
* Short ADR (architecture decision record) in `/docs/adr/` * Follow-up tickets with sized estimates * If we decide to build, a tracking Epic
*Time-box:* 3 working days. Owner: Bartek Nowak.

item.title: [PROJ-21] Improve checkout: invite teammates by email
item.summary: Improve checkout: invite teammates by email
item.description:
h2. Goal
Enable admin to configure SSO with Okta, so that the team has visibility. Today this is painful because users have to work around it by chaining multiple manual steps, which creates support load and makes the product feel inconsistent.
h2. User story
> As a admin, > I want to configure SSO with Okta, > so that the team has visibility.
h2. Acceptance criteria
* The notifications service exposes a first-class UI for this action * Feature is gated behind a feature flag `ff_proj-21` rolled out at 5% → 25% → 100% * Empty-state, loading, success and error paths are all covered * Copy reviewed by the product writer and available in English + Polish * Telemetry: at least one success event and one failure event tracked
h2. Out of scope
* Mobile parity (tracked separately) * Bulk operations on more than 100 items * Admin-level permissions for this action
h2. Open questions
# Do we need an audit trail entry for every use of this action? # Should failure notifications go to the user, the admin, or both? # What's the expected rate limit? Current proposal: 50 per user per minute.
h2. Design & references
* Figma: _notifications service v2 → audit log_ frame * Linked OKR: _Reduce time-to-value for new accounts_ * Related RFC: RFC-836 (notifications service audit log)
*Estimated effort:* 8 story points. Target sprint: the one after kickoff.

comments.comment: Reproduced locally on commit abc123. Working on a fix in branch fix/proj-21.
Closing as duplicate of related ticket — same root cause.

item.title: [PROJ-22] [Bug] Allow developer to preview changes before publish
item.summary: [Bug] Allow developer to preview changes before publish
item.description:
h2. Mission
Consolidate how developer interact with OpenTelemetry tracing across the graph indexer. Today the experience is fragmented because this surface has accumulated 3+ years of ad-hoc additions. Our goal is a coherent design with clear contracts, measurable adoption, and a migration path that doesn't leave old integrations stranded.
h2. Success metrics
* Adoption: ≥ 40% of active accounts using the new surface within 60 days of GA * Support load: −30% tickets tagged `graph indexer` within 90 days * Performance: p95 latency on the hot path ≤ 150ms * Reliability: no new P1 incidents attributed to this work in the first two quarters
h2. Workstreams
# **API & data model** – new contract, versioning, deprecation policy # **UX** – redesign of the primary flow, empty/loading/error states # **Migration** – dual-write, backfill, cutover, cleanup # **Observability** – dashboards, SLOs, on-call runbook # **Enablement** – docs for internal teams + external API consumers
h2. Non-goals
* Rewriting the underlying storage layer * Adding new surface-level features during migration * Mobile parity in phase 1
h2. Timeline (target)
* Week 1–2: discovery + RFC-142 * Week 3–6: build behind `ff_proj-22` flag * Week 7: internal dogfood * Week 8–9: beta at 5% of MAU * Week 10: GA + announcement
*Estimated effort:* 5 story points total across squads. Primary contact: Iwona Lewandowska.

item.description:
h2. Context
We noticed memory leak under load in the rate limiter affecting guest user across the staging environment. Impact started around 2026-03-30, correlating with the v4.12 release. At peak we observed roughly 2.4% error rate on the affected endpoints, with users reporting slow response times and occasional 5xx errors. Support ticket volume doubled during the incident window.
h2. Reproduction steps
# Open the rate limiter in a clean session on staging # Trigger the flow that calls POST /api/v1/webhooks/dispatch # Apply moderate load (~500 req/s sustained for 5 minutes) # Observe latency spike and intermittent memory leak under load
h2. Acceptance criteria
* memory leak under load reproduced locally with a deterministic test harness * Root cause identified and captured in a short RFC * Regression test added that fails on main before the fix and passes after * Dashboards in Grafana updated so we'd detect this before customers do * Runbook entry in rate limiter runbook references this ticket
h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at the migration job that runs every night at 02:00 UTC. We should check the latest changes in rate limiter that touched the POST /api/v1/webhooks/dispatch handler. Hiro Tanaka already pulled flamegraphs from staging; they're attached in the incident channel.
h2. Links
* Incident channel: #inc-proj-27 * Related dashboards: Grafana > rate limiter > Overview * Previous similar issue: see linked tickets
*Estimated effort:* 5 story points. Discussed in the 2026-03-30 sync. Owner: Hiro Tanaka.

comments.comment: Bumping priority — customer escalation came in this morning.

item.title: [OPS-24] Add OpenTelemetry tracing to graph indexer
item.summary: Add OpenTelemetry tracing to graph indexer
item.description:
h2. Goal
Enable support agent to rotate API keys without downtime, so that we reduce support load. Today this is painful because users have to work around it by chaining multiple manual steps, which creates support load and makes the product feel inconsistent.
h2. User story
> As a support agent, > I want to rotate API keys without downtime, > so that we reduce support load.
h2. Acceptance criteria
* The search pipeline exposes a first-class UI for this action * Feature is gated behind a feature flag `ff_ops-24` rolled out at 5% → 25% → 100% * Empty-state, loading, success and error paths are all covered * Copy reviewed by the product writer and available in English + Polish * Telemetry: at least one success event and one failure event tracked
h2. Out of scope
* Mobile parity (tracked separately) * Bulk operations on more than 100 items * Admin-level permissions for this action
h2. Open questions
# Do we need an audit trail entry for every use of this action? # Should failure notifications go to the user, the admin, or both? # What's the expected rate limit? Current proposal: 50 per user per minute.
h2. Design & references
* Figma: _search pipeline v2 → graceful shutdown_ frame * Linked OKR: _Reduce time-to-value for new accounts_ * Related RFC: RFC-426 (search pipeline graceful shutdown)
*Estimated effort:* 5 story points. Target sprint: the one after kickoff.

item.title: [OPS-25] Investigate race condition in worker pool in auth flow
item.summary: Investigate race condition in worker pool in auth flow
item.description:
h2. Mission
Consolidate how end user interact with audit log across the rate limiter. Today the experience is fragmented because this surface has accumulated 3+ years of ad-hoc additions. Our goal is a coherent design with clear contracts, measurable adoption, and a migration path that doesn't leave old integrations stranded.
h2. Success metrics
* Adoption: ≥ 40% of active accounts using the new surface within 60 days of GA * Support load: −30% tickets tagged `rate limiter` within 90 days * Performance: p95 latency on the hot path ≤ 150ms * Reliability: no new P1 incidents attributed to this work in the first two quarters
h2. Workstreams
# **API & data model** – new contract, versioning, deprecation policy # **UX** – redesign of the primary flow, empty/loading/error states # **Migration** – dual-write, backfill, cutover, cleanup # **Observability** – dashboards, SLOs, on-call runbook # **Enablement** – docs for internal teams + external API consumers
h2. Non-goals
* Rewriting the underlying storage layer * Adding new surface-level features during migration * Mobile parity in phase 1
h2. Timeline (target)
* Week 1–2: discovery + RFC-429 * Week 3–6: build behind `ff_ops-25` flag * Week 7: internal dogfood * Week 8–9: beta at 15% of MAU * Week 10: GA + announcement
*Estimated effort:* 3 story points total across squads. Primary contact: Bartek Nowak.

comments.comment: Closing as duplicate of related ticket — same root cause.

item.title: [PROJ-28] [Bug] Refactor embedding cache for we meet compliance requirements
item.summary: [Bug] Refactor embedding cache for we meet compliance requirements
item.description:
h2. Spike goal
Evaluate options for keyboard shortcuts in the checkout and land with a clear recommendation. Current pain: duplicate webhook deliveries is surfacing in staging at ~0.5% rate, and ad-hoc fixes aren't sticking. We need a durable direction before committing engineering time.
h2. Questions to answer
# What's the smallest viable change that would reduce duplicate webhook deliveries by at least 50%? # Can we reuse something from the checkout or do we need new infra? # What's the operational cost (on-call, observability, cost per request)? # Are there open-source projects we should evaluate before building?
h2. Options considered
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Est. effort || | Build in-house | full control, fits our stack | maintenance cost, longer ship time | 13 pts | | Use OSS (OpenTelemetry Collector) | faster to prototype, active community | tuning required, unclear SLAs | 2 pts | | SaaS (Vercel) | least upfront effort | vendor lock-in, PII and compliance | 3 pts | | Do nothing | zero cost | pain persists, risk escalates | n/a |
h2. Validation plan
* Prototype the top option in a throwaway branch * Run load test at 50 req/s for 30 minutes on staging * Collect p50/p95/p99 latency and memory + CPU usage * Share findings in the 2026-04-12 architecture review
h2. Output
* Short ADR (architecture decision record) in `/docs/adr/` * Follow-up tickets with sized estimates * If we decide to build, a tracking Epic
*Time-box:* 3 working days. Owner: Iwona Lewandowska.

item.title: [OPS-27] [Bug] As a developer, I want to preview changes before publish so that we save costs
item.summary: [Bug] As a developer, I want to preview changes before publish so that we save costs
item.description:
h2. Summary
Tracking implementation work for feature flags in the websocket layer. This is part of the broader effort to I can move faster. The current implementation is a stop-gap from the v5.2-hotfix release and needs to be replaced before we onboard the next cohort.
h2. Plan
# Audit current behavior and write down the invariants we want to preserve # Draft a short RFC (RFC-689) covering data model, API contract, rollback path # Implement behind the `ff_ops-27` feature flag with a kill switch # Cover new code with unit + integration tests; target ≥ 80% branch coverage # Roll out to the beta cohort (~10% of MAU) for 14 days # GA after 14 days of stability and no new P1 incidents
h2. Technical design notes
We considered three approaches:
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Decision || | Extend existing websocket layer | fastest to ship, reuses patterns | couples us to legacy code | | | Extract a dedicated service | clean seams, independent scaling | infra cost, more on-call | | | Use a SaaS primitive | least effort | vendor lock-in, PII concerns | rejected |
Default pick: **extend existing websocket layer** unless profiling on staging shows we can't hit the 1000 req/s target with p95 latency under 200ms.
h2. Risks & mitigations
* **Data migration:** writes double-written during rollout. Backfill job gated by flag. * **On-call load:** new dashboards + runbook entries land in the same PR as the feature. * **Rollback:** feature flag flip restores old path within seconds.
*Estimated effort:* 3 story points. Dependencies: platform team's IAM work.

# What's the smallest viable change that would reduce TLS handshake timeouts by at least 50%? # Can we reuse something from the migration scripts or do we need new infra? # What's the operational cost (on-call, observability, cost per request)? # Are there open-source projects we should evaluate before building?
h2. Options considered
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Est. effort || | Build in-house | full control, fits our stack | maintenance cost, longer ship time | 21 pts | | Use OSS (LiteFS) | faster to prototype, active community | tuning required, unclear SLAs | 2 pts | | SaaS (Auth0) | least upfront effort | vendor lock-in, PII and compliance | 1 pts | | Do nothing | zero cost | pain persists, risk escalates | n/a |
h2. Validation plan
* Prototype the top option in a throwaway branch * Run load test at 500 req/s for 30 minutes on staging * Collect p50/p95/p99 latency and memory + CPU usage * Share findings in the 2026-04-15 architecture review
h2. Output
* Short ADR (architecture decision record) in `/docs/adr/` * Follow-up tickets with sized estimates * If we decide to build, a tracking Epic
*Time-box:* 3 working days. Owner: Bartek Nowak.

comments.comment: Bumping priority — customer escalation came in this morning.
Reproduced locally on commit abc123. Working on a fix in branch fix/ops-31.

item.title: [PROJ-38] As a end user, I want to export reports as CSV so that I can move faster
item.summary: As a end user, I want to export reports as CSV so that I can move faster
item.description:
h2. Goal
Enable support agent to see activity history per workspace, so that users trust the system. Today this is painful because users have to work around it by chaining multiple manual steps, which creates support load and makes the product feel inconsistent.
h2. User story
> As a support agent, > I want to see activity history per workspace, > so that users trust the system.
h2. Acceptance criteria
* The auth flow exposes a first-class UI for this action * Feature is gated behind a feature flag `experiment_proj-38` rolled out at 5% → 25% → 100% * Empty-state, loading, success and error paths are all covered * Copy reviewed by the product writer and available in English + Polish * Telemetry: at least one success event and one failure event tracked
h2. Out of scope
* Mobile parity (tracked separately) * Bulk operations on more than 100 items * Admin-level permissions for this action
h2. Open questions
# Do we need an audit trail entry for every use of this action? # Should failure notifications go to the user, the admin, or both? # What's the expected rate limit? Current proposal: 250 per user per minute.
h2. Design & references
* Figma: _auth flow v2 → graceful shutdown_ frame * Linked OKR: _Reduce time-to-value for new accounts_ * Related RFC: RFC-151 (auth flow graceful shutdown)
*Estimated effort:* 3 story points. Target sprint: the one after kickoff.

item.title: [OPS-33] [Bug] Refactor migration scripts for we meet compliance requirements
item.summary: [Bug] Refactor migration scripts for we meet compliance requirements
item.description:
h2. Summary
Tracking implementation work for feature flags in the auth flow. This is part of the broader effort to the team has visibility. The current implementation is a stop-gap from the v5.1 release and needs to be replaced before we onboard the next cohort.
h2. Plan
# Audit current behavior and write down the invariants we want to preserve # Draft a short RFC (RFC-341) covering data model, API contract, rollback path # Implement behind the `ff_ops-33` feature flag with a kill switch # Cover new code with unit + integration tests; target ≥ 80% branch coverage # Roll out to the beta cohort (~25% of MAU) for 14 days # GA after 14 days of stability and no new P1 incidents
h2. Technical design notes
We considered three approaches:
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Decision || | Extend existing auth flow | fastest to ship, reuses patterns | couples us to legacy code | | | Extract a dedicated service | clean seams, independent scaling | infra cost, more on-call | | | Use a SaaS primitive | least effort | vendor lock-in, PII concerns | rejected |
Default pick: **extend existing auth flow** unless profiling on staging shows we can't hit the 500 req/s target with p95 latency under 200ms.
h2. Risks & mitigations
* **Data migration:** writes double-written during rollout. Backfill job gated by flag. * **On-call load:** new dashboards + runbook entries land in the same PR as the feature. * **Rollback:** feature flag flip restores old path within seconds.
*Estimated effort:* 21 story points. Dependencies: platform team's IAM work.

comments.comment: Discussed offline. We'll defer this until after the migration.

item.title: [OPS-35] Allow admin to schedule recurring jobs
item.summary: Allow admin to schedule recurring jobs
item.description:
h2. Context
We noticed slow queries on indexed columns in the checkout affecting support agent across the eu-west-1 environment. Impact started around 2026-04-02, correlating with the v5.1 release. At peak we observed roughly 4.8% error rate on the affected endpoints, with users reporting slow response times and occasional 5xx errors. Support ticket volume doubled during the incident window.
h2. Reproduction steps
# Open the checkout in a clean session on eu-west-1 # Trigger the flow that calls PATCH /api/v2/billing/subscription # Apply moderate load (~500 req/s sustained for 5 minutes) # Observe latency spike and intermittent slow queries on indexed columns
h2. Acceptance criteria
* slow queries on indexed columns reproduced locally with a deterministic test harness * Root cause identified and captured in a short RFC * Regression test added that fails on main before the fix and passes after * Dashboards in Grafana updated so we'd detect this before customers do * Runbook entry in checkout runbook references this ticket
h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at a lock ordering change landed two weeks ago. We should check the latest changes in checkout that touched the PATCH /api/v2/billing/subscription handler. Chloé Martin already pulled flamegraphs from eu-west-1; they're attached in the incident channel.
h2. Links
* Incident channel: #inc-ops-35 * Related dashboards: Grafana > checkout > Overview * Previous similar issue: see linked tickets
*Estimated effort:* 21 story points. Discussed in the 2026-04-02 sync. Owner: Chloé Martin.

comments.comment: Closing as duplicate of related ticket — same root cause.

|| Option || Pros || Cons || Est. effort || | Build in-house | full control, fits our stack | maintenance cost, longer ship time | 3 pts | | Use OSS (Vector.dev) | faster to prototype, active community | tuning required, unclear SLAs | 3 pts | | SaaS (Datadog) | least upfront effort | vendor lock-in, PII and compliance | 3 pts | | Do nothing | zero cost | pain persists, risk escalates | n/a |
h2. Validation plan
* Prototype the top option in a throwaway branch * Run load test at 250 req/s for 30 minutes on staging * Collect p50/p95/p99 latency and memory + CPU usage * Share findings in the 2026-03-29 architecture review
h2. Output
* Short ADR (architecture decision record) in `/docs/adr/` * Follow-up tickets with sized estimates * If we decide to build, a tracking Epic
*Time-box:* 3 working days. Owner: Eva Schmidt.

Tracking implementation work for OpenTelemetry tracing in the graph indexer. This is part of the broader effort to users trust the system. The current implementation is a stop-gap from the v4.13-rc1 release and needs to be replaced before we onboard the next cohort.
h2. Plan
# Audit current behavior and write down the invariants we want to preserve # Draft a short RFC (RFC-623) covering data model, API contract, rollback path # Implement behind the `rollout_ops-39` feature flag with a kill switch # Cover new code with unit + integration tests; target ≥ 80% branch coverage # Roll out to the beta cohort (~15% of MAU) for 14 days # GA after 14 days of stability and no new P1 incidents
h2. Technical design notes
We considered three approaches:
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Decision || | Extend existing graph indexer | fastest to ship, reuses patterns | couples us to legacy code | | | Extract a dedicated service | clean seams, independent scaling | infra cost, more on-call | | | Use a SaaS primitive | least effort | vendor lock-in, PII concerns | rejected |
Default pick: **extend existing graph indexer** unless profiling on staging shows we can't hit the 2500 req/s target with p95 latency under 200ms.
h2. Risks & mitigations
* **Data migration:** writes double-written during rollout. Backfill job gated by flag. * **On-call load:** new dashboards + runbook entries land in the same PR as the feature. * **Rollback:** feature flag flip restores old path within seconds.
*Estimated effort:* 13 story points. Dependencies: platform team's IAM work.

item.title: [PROJ-49] As a developer, I want to configure SSO with Okta so that we reduce support load
item.summary: As a developer, I want to configure SSO with Okta so that we reduce support load
item.description:
h2. Goal
Enable billing manager to preview changes before publish, so that I can move faster. Today this is painful because users have to work around it by chaining multiple manual steps, which creates support load and makes the product feel inconsistent.
h2. User story
> As a billing manager, > I want to preview changes before publish, > so that I can move faster.
h2. Acceptance criteria
* The auth flow exposes a first-class UI for this action * Feature is gated behind a feature flag `experiment_proj-49` rolled out at 5% → 25% → 100% * Empty-state, loading, success and error paths are all covered * Copy reviewed by the product writer and available in English + Polish * Telemetry: at least one success event and one failure event tracked
h2. Out of scope
* Mobile parity (tracked separately) * Bulk operations on more than 100 items * Admin-level permissions for this action
h2. Open questions
# Do we need an audit trail entry for every use of this action? # Should failure notifications go to the user, the admin, or both? # What's the expected rate limit? Current proposal: 2500 per user per minute.
h2. Design & references
* Figma: _auth flow v2 → API rate-limit headers_ frame * Linked OKR: _Reduce time-to-value for new accounts_ * Related RFC: RFC-834 (auth flow API rate-limit headers)
*Estimated effort:* 3 story points. Target sprint: the one after kickoff.

item.title: [PROJ-50] Improve rate limiter: invite teammates by email
item.summary: Improve rate limiter: invite teammates by email
item.description:
h2. Spike goal
Evaluate options for PII redaction in the rate limiter and land with a clear recommendation. Current pain: drift between primary and replica is surfacing in staging at ~12.5% rate, and ad-hoc fixes aren't sticking. We need a durable direction before committing engineering time.
h2. Questions to answer
# What's the smallest viable change that would reduce drift between primary and replica by at least 50%? # Can we reuse something from the rate limiter or do we need new infra? # What's the operational cost (on-call, observability, cost per request)? # Are there open-source projects we should evaluate before building?
h2. Options considered
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Est. effort || | Build in-house | full control, fits our stack | maintenance cost, longer ship time | 8 pts | | Use OSS (Keda) | faster to prototype, active community | tuning required, unclear SLAs | 3 pts | | SaaS (Datadog) | least upfront effort | vendor lock-in, PII and compliance | 3 pts | | Do nothing | zero cost | pain persists, risk escalates | n/a |
h2. Validation plan
* Prototype the top option in a throwaway branch * Run load test at 1000 req/s for 30 minutes on staging * Collect p50/p95/p99 latency and memory + CPU usage * Share findings in the 2026-04-02 architecture review
h2. Output
* Short ADR (architecture decision record) in `/docs/adr/` * Follow-up tickets with sized estimates * If we decide to build, a tracking Epic
*Time-box:* 3 working days. Owner: Jakub Wiśniewski.

comments.comment: Bumping priority — customer escalation came in this morning.

item.title: [OPS-41] Add API rate-limit headers to embedding cache
item.summary: Add API rate-limit headers to embedding cache
item.description:
h2. Mission
Consolidate how guest user interact with keyboard shortcuts across the checkout. Today the experience is fragmented because this surface has accumulated 3+ years of ad-hoc additions. Our goal is a coherent design with clear contracts, measurable adoption, and a migration path that doesn't leave old integrations stranded.
h2. Success metrics
* Adoption: ≥ 40% of active accounts using the new surface within 60 days of GA * Support load: −30% tickets tagged `checkout` within 90 days * Performance: p95 latency on the hot path ≤ 150ms * Reliability: no new P1 incidents attributed to this work in the first two quarters
h2. Workstreams
# **API & data model** – new contract, versioning, deprecation policy # **UX** – redesign of the primary flow, empty/loading/error states # **Migration** – dual-write, backfill, cutover, cleanup # **Observability** – dashboards, SLOs, on-call runbook # **Enablement** – docs for internal teams + external API consumers
h2. Non-goals
* Rewriting the underlying storage layer * Adding new surface-level features during migration * Mobile parity in phase 1
h2. Timeline (target)
* Week 1–2: discovery + RFC-370 * Week 3–6: build behind `rollout_ops-41` flag * Week 7: internal dogfood * Week 8–9: beta at 15% of MAU * Week 10: GA + announcement
*Estimated effort:* 5 story points total across squads. Primary contact: Grace Liu.

comments.comment: Reproduced locally on commit abc123. Working on a fix in branch fix/ops-41.
Pushed a draft PR — would appreciate eyes on the lock acquisition order.

item.title: [OPS-45] Add feature flags to embedding cache
item.summary: Add feature flags to embedding cache
item.description:
h2. Context
We noticed TLS handshake timeouts in the websocket layer affecting billing manager across the eu-west-1 environment. Impact started around 2026-03-17, correlating with the v5.1 release. At peak we observed roughly 7.1% error rate on the affected endpoints, with users reporting slow response times and occasional 5xx errors. Support ticket volume doubled during the incident window.
h2. Reproduction steps
# Open the websocket layer in a clean session on eu-west-1 # Trigger the flow that calls PATCH /api/v2/billing/subscription # Apply moderate load (~50 req/s sustained for 5 minutes) # Observe latency spike and intermittent TLS handshake timeouts
h2. Acceptance criteria
* TLS handshake timeouts reproduced locally with a deterministic test harness * Root cause identified and captured in a short RFC * Regression test added that fails on main before the fix and passes after * Dashboards in Grafana updated so we'd detect this before customers do * Runbook entry in websocket layer runbook references this ticket
h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at a lock ordering change landed two weeks ago. We should check the latest changes in websocket layer that touched the PATCH /api/v2/billing/subscription handler. Daniel O'Brien already pulled flamegraphs from eu-west-1; they're attached in the incident channel.
h2. Links
* Incident channel: #inc-ops-45 * Related dashboards: Grafana > websocket layer > Overview * Previous similar issue: see linked tickets
*Estimated effort:* 13 story points. Discussed in the 2026-03-17 sync. Owner: Daniel O'Brien.

comments.comment: Discussed offline. We'll defer this until after the migration.
Closing as duplicate of related ticket — same root cause.

item.summary: As a end user, I want to invite teammates by email so that we meet compliance requirements
item.description:
h2. Context
We noticed OOM in batch job in the checkout affecting admin across the canary environment. Impact started around 2026-04-12, correlating with the v5.1 release. At peak we observed roughly 12.5% error rate on the affected endpoints, with users reporting slow response times and occasional 5xx errors. Support ticket volume doubled during the incident window.
h2. Reproduction steps
# Open the checkout in a clean session on canary # Trigger the flow that calls POST /api/v2/workspaces # Apply moderate load (~100 req/s sustained for 5 minutes) # Observe latency spike and intermittent OOM in batch job
h2. Acceptance criteria
* OOM in batch job reproduced locally with a deterministic test harness * Root cause identified and captured in a short RFC * Regression test added that fails on main before the fix and passes after * Dashboards in Grafana updated so we'd detect this before customers do * Runbook entry in checkout runbook references this ticket
h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at the migration job that runs every night at 02:00 UTC. We should check the latest changes in checkout that touched the POST /api/v2/workspaces handler. Anna Kowalska already pulled flamegraphs from canary; they're attached in the incident channel.
h2. Links

item.title: [PROJ-54] [Bug] Investigate drift between primary and replica in embedding cache
item.summary: [Bug] Investigate drift between primary and replica in embedding cache
item.description:
h2. Goal
Enable billing manager to rotate API keys without downtime, so that we reduce support load. Today this is painful because users have to work around it by chaining multiple manual steps, which creates support load and makes the product feel inconsistent.
h2. User story
> As a billing manager, > I want to rotate API keys without downtime, > so that we reduce support load.
h2. Acceptance criteria
* The sync engine exposes a first-class UI for this action * Feature is gated behind a feature flag `rollout_proj-54` rolled out at 5% → 25% → 100% * Empty-state, loading, success and error paths are all covered * Copy reviewed by the product writer and available in English + Polish * Telemetry: at least one success event and one failure event tracked
h2. Out of scope
* Mobile parity (tracked separately) * Bulk operations on more than 100 items * Admin-level permissions for this action
h2. Open questions
# Do we need an audit trail entry for every use of this action? # Should failure notifications go to the user, the admin, or both? # What's the expected rate limit? Current proposal: 50 per user per minute.
h2. Design & references
* Figma: _sync engine v2 → dark mode_ frame * Linked OKR: _Reduce time-to-value for new accounts_ * Related RFC: RFC-132 (sync engine dark mode)
*Estimated effort:* 3 story points. Target sprint: the one after kickoff.

item.title: [OPS-52] As a developer, I want to configure SSO with Okta so that the team has visibility
item.summary: As a developer, I want to configure SSO with Okta so that the team has visibility
item.description:
h2. Mission
Consolidate how billing manager interact with dark mode across the websocket layer. Today the experience is fragmented because this surface has accumulated 3+ years of ad-hoc additions. Our goal is a coherent design with clear contracts, measurable adoption, and a migration path that doesn't leave old integrations stranded.
h2. Success metrics
* Adoption: ≥ 40% of active accounts using the new surface within 60 days of GA * Support load: −30% tickets tagged `websocket layer` within 90 days * Performance: p95 latency on the hot path ≤ 150ms * Reliability: no new P1 incidents attributed to this work in the first two quarters
h2. Workstreams
# **API & data model** – new contract, versioning, deprecation policy # **UX** – redesign of the primary flow, empty/loading/error states # **Migration** – dual-write, backfill, cutover, cleanup # **Observability** – dashboards, SLOs, on-call runbook # **Enablement** – docs for internal teams + external API consumers
h2. Non-goals
* Rewriting the underlying storage layer * Adding new surface-level features during migration * Mobile parity in phase 1
h2. Timeline (target)
* Week 1–2: discovery + RFC-684 * Week 3–6: build behind `rollout_ops-52` flag * Week 7: internal dogfood * Week 8–9: beta at 25% of MAU * Week 10: GA + announcement
*Estimated effort:* 5 story points total across squads. Primary contact: Grace Liu.

item.title: [OPS-53] Fix intermittent 502s affecting admin
item.summary: Fix intermittent 502s affecting admin
item.description:
h2. Context
We noticed OOM in batch job in the rate limiter affecting developer across the eu-west-1 environment. Impact started around 2026-04-12, correlating with the v5.1 release. At peak we observed roughly 7.1% error rate on the affected endpoints, with users reporting slow response times and occasional 5xx errors. Support ticket volume doubled during the incident window.
h2. Reproduction steps
# Open the rate limiter in a clean session on eu-west-1 # Trigger the flow that calls POST /api/v1/webhooks/dispatch # Apply moderate load (~1000 req/s sustained for 5 minutes) # Observe latency spike and intermittent OOM in batch job
h2. Acceptance criteria
* OOM in batch job reproduced locally with a deterministic test harness * Root cause identified and captured in a short RFC * Regression test added that fails on main before the fix and passes after * Dashboards in Grafana updated so we'd detect this before customers do * Runbook entry in rate limiter runbook references this ticket
h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at a lock ordering change landed two weeks ago. We should check the latest changes in rate limiter that touched the POST /api/v1/webhooks/dispatch handler. Grace Liu already pulled flamegraphs from eu-west-1; they're attached in the incident channel.
h2. Links
* Incident channel: #inc-ops-53 * Related dashboards: Grafana > rate limiter > Overview * Previous similar issue: see linked tickets
*Estimated effort:* 5 story points. Discussed in the 2026-04-12 sync. Owner: Grace Liu.

comments.comment: This is blocked on the platform team finishing the new IAM roles.

item.title: [PROJ-55] Add feature flags to sync engine
item.summary: Add feature flags to sync engine
item.description:
h2. Summary
Tracking implementation work for structured logging in the websocket layer. This is part of the broader effort to we reduce support load. The current implementation is a stop-gap from the v4.12 release and needs to be replaced before we onboard the next cohort.
h2. Plan
# Audit current behavior and write down the invariants we want to preserve # Draft a short RFC (RFC-973) covering data model, API contract, rollback path # Implement behind the `ff_proj-55` feature flag with a kill switch # Cover new code with unit + integration tests; target ≥ 80% branch coverage # Roll out to the beta cohort (~5% of MAU) for 14 days # GA after 14 days of stability and no new P1 incidents
h2. Technical design notes
We considered three approaches:
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Decision || | Extend existing websocket layer | fastest to ship, reuses patterns | couples us to legacy code | | | Extract a dedicated service | clean seams, independent scaling | infra cost, more on-call | | | Use a SaaS primitive | least effort | vendor lock-in, PII concerns | rejected |
Default pick: **extend existing websocket layer** unless profiling on staging shows we can't hit the 100 req/s target with p95 latency under 200ms.
h2. Risks & mitigations
* **Data migration:** writes double-written during rollout. Backfill job gated by flag. * **On-call load:** new dashboards + runbook entries land in the same PR as the feature. * **Rollback:** feature flag flip restores old path within seconds.
*Estimated effort:* 13 story points. Dependencies: platform team's IAM work.

comments.comment: This is blocked on the platform team finishing the new IAM roles.

Pushed a draft PR — would appreciate eyes on the lock acquisition order.

item.title: [OPS-55] Investigate intermittent 502s in embedding cache
item.summary: Investigate intermittent 502s in embedding cache
item.description:
h2. Context
We noticed OOM in batch job in the notifications service affecting support agent across the staging environment. Impact started around 2026-04-04, correlating with the v4.13-rc1 release. At peak we observed roughly 2.4% error rate on the affected endpoints, with users reporting slow response times and occasional 5xx errors. Support ticket volume doubled during the incident window.
h2. Reproduction steps
# Open the notifications service in a clean session on staging # Trigger the flow that calls PATCH /api/v2/billing/subscription # Apply moderate load (~500 req/s sustained for 5 minutes) # Observe latency spike and intermittent OOM in batch job
h2. Acceptance criteria
* OOM in batch job reproduced locally with a deterministic test harness * Root cause identified and captured in a short RFC * Regression test added that fails on main before the fix and passes after * Dashboards in Grafana updated so we'd detect this before customers do * Runbook entry in notifications service runbook references this ticket
h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at an undocumented assumption about clock monotonicity. We should check the latest changes in notifications service that touched the PATCH /api/v2/billing/subscription handler. Anna Kowalska already pulled flamegraphs from staging; they're attached in the incident channel.
h2. Links
* Incident channel: #inc-ops-55 * Related dashboards: Grafana > notifications service > Overview * Previous similar issue: see linked tickets
*Estimated effort:* 21 story points. Discussed in the 2026-04-04 sync. Owner: Anna Kowalska.

h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at the recently introduced connection-pool tuning. We should check the latest changes in checkout that touched the POST /api/v2/workspaces handler. Bartek Nowak already pulled flamegraphs from eu-west-1; they're attached in the incident channel.

* Incident channel: #inc-proj-58 * Related dashboards: Grafana > checkout > Overview * Previous similar issue: see linked tickets

Consolidate how admin interact with PII redaction across the auth flow. Today the experience is fragmented because this surface has accumulated 3+ years of ad-hoc additions. Our goal is a coherent design with clear contracts, measurable adoption, and a migration path that doesn't leave old integrations stranded.
h2. Success metrics
* Adoption: ≥ 40% of active accounts using the new surface within 60 days of GA * Support load: −30% tickets tagged `auth flow` within 90 days * Performance: p95 latency on the hot path ≤ 150ms * Reliability: no new P1 incidents attributed to this work in the first two quarters
h2. Workstreams
# **API & data model** – new contract, versioning, deprecation policy # **UX** – redesign of the primary flow, empty/loading/error states # **Migration** – dual-write, backfill, cutover, cleanup # **Observability** – dashboards, SLOs, on-call runbook # **Enablement** – docs for internal teams + external API consumers
h2. Non-goals
* Rewriting the underlying storage layer * Adding new surface-level features during migration * Mobile parity in phase 1
h2. Timeline (target)
* Week 1–2: discovery + RFC-897 * Week 3–6: build behind `ff_proj-62` flag * Week 7: internal dogfood * Week 8–9: beta at 5% of MAU * Week 10: GA + announcement
*Estimated effort:* 3 story points total across squads. Primary contact: Iwona Lewandowska.

item.title: [OPS-61] [Bug] Fix drift between primary and replica affecting guest user
item.summary: [Bug] Fix drift between primary and replica affecting guest user
item.description:
h2. Goal
Enable billing manager to export reports as CSV, so that we reduce support load. Today this is painful because users have to work around it by chaining multiple manual steps, which creates support load and makes the product feel inconsistent.
h2. User story
> As a billing manager, > I want to export reports as CSV, > so that we reduce support load.
h2. Acceptance criteria
* The graph indexer exposes a first-class UI for this action * Feature is gated behind a feature flag `experiment_ops-61` rolled out at 5% → 25% → 100% * Empty-state, loading, success and error paths are all covered * Copy reviewed by the product writer and available in English + Polish * Telemetry: at least one success event and one failure event tracked
h2. Out of scope
* Mobile parity (tracked separately) * Bulk operations on more than 100 items * Admin-level permissions for this action
h2. Open questions
# Do we need an audit trail entry for every use of this action? # Should failure notifications go to the user, the admin, or both? # What's the expected rate limit? Current proposal: 1000 per user per minute.
h2. Design & references
* Figma: _graph indexer v2 → graceful shutdown_ frame * Linked OKR: _Reduce time-to-value for new accounts_ * Related RFC: RFC-927 (graph indexer graceful shutdown)
*Estimated effort:* 8 story points. Target sprint: the one after kickoff.

comments.comment: Verified in staging. Ready for production rollout.

item.title: [PROJ-63] Refactor sync engine for we save costs
item.summary: Refactor sync engine for we save costs
item.description:
h2. Spike goal
Evaluate options for OpenTelemetry tracing in the search pipeline and land with a clear recommendation. Current pain: memory leak under load is surfacing in canary at ~2.4% rate, and ad-hoc fixes aren't sticking. We need a durable direction before committing engineering time.
h2. Questions to answer
# What's the smallest viable change that would reduce memory leak under load by at least 50%? # Can we reuse something from the search pipeline or do we need new infra? # What's the operational cost (on-call, observability, cost per request)? # Are there open-source projects we should evaluate before building?
h2. Options considered
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Est. effort || | Build in-house | full control, fits our stack | maintenance cost, longer ship time | 13 pts | | Use OSS (Loki) | faster to prototype, active community | tuning required, unclear SLAs | 5 pts | | SaaS (Vercel) | least upfront effort | vendor lock-in, PII and compliance | 3 pts | | Do nothing | zero cost | pain persists, risk escalates | n/a |
h2. Validation plan
* Prototype the top option in a throwaway branch * Run load test at 250 req/s for 30 minutes on staging * Collect p50/p95/p99 latency and memory + CPU usage * Share findings in the 2026-03-27 architecture review
h2. Output
* Short ADR (architecture decision record) in `/docs/adr/` * Follow-up tickets with sized estimates * If we decide to build, a tracking Epic
*Time-box:* 3 working days. Owner: Eva Schmidt.

item.title: [PROJ-64] Add dark mode to notifications service
item.summary: Add dark mode to notifications service
item.description:
h2. Context
We noticed slow queries on indexed columns in the sync engine affecting admin across the eu-west-1 environment. Impact started around 2026-03-20, correlating with the v4.12 release. At peak we observed roughly 12.5% error rate on the affected endpoints, with users reporting slow response times and occasional 5xx errors. Support ticket volume doubled during the incident window.
h2. Reproduction steps
# Open the sync engine in a clean session on eu-west-1 # Trigger the flow that calls POST /api/v1/auth/token # Apply moderate load (~500 req/s sustained for 5 minutes) # Observe latency spike and intermittent slow queries on indexed columns
h2. Acceptance criteria
* slow queries on indexed columns reproduced locally with a deterministic test harness * Root cause identified and captured in a short RFC * Regression test added that fails on main before the fix and passes after * Dashboards in Grafana updated so we'd detect this before customers do * Runbook entry in sync engine runbook references this ticket
h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at the migration job that runs every night at 02:00 UTC. We should check the latest changes in sync engine that touched the POST /api/v1/auth/token handler. Bartek Nowak already pulled flamegraphs from eu-west-1; they're attached in the incident channel.
h2. Links
* Incident channel: #inc-proj-64 * Related dashboards: Grafana > sync engine > Overview * Previous similar issue: see linked tickets
*Estimated effort:* 5 story points. Discussed in the 2026-03-20 sync. Owner: Bartek Nowak.

comments.comment: This is blocked on the platform team finishing the new IAM roles.

item.title: [OPS-62] Investigate memory leak under load in auth flow
item.summary: Investigate memory leak under load in auth flow
item.description:
h2. Summary
Tracking implementation work for audit log in the checkout. This is part of the broader effort to we meet compliance requirements. The current implementation is a stop-gap from the v5.2-hotfix release and needs to be replaced before we onboard the next cohort.
h2. Plan
# Audit current behavior and write down the invariants we want to preserve # Draft a short RFC (RFC-513) covering data model, API contract, rollback path # Implement behind the `experiment_ops-62` feature flag with a kill switch # Cover new code with unit + integration tests; target ≥ 80% branch coverage # Roll out to the beta cohort (~5% of MAU) for 14 days # GA after 14 days of stability and no new P1 incidents
h2. Technical design notes
We considered three approaches:
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Decision || | Extend existing checkout | fastest to ship, reuses patterns | couples us to legacy code | | | Extract a dedicated service | clean seams, independent scaling | infra cost, more on-call | | | Use a SaaS primitive | least effort | vendor lock-in, PII concerns | rejected |
Default pick: **extend existing checkout** unless profiling on staging shows we can't hit the 1000 req/s target with p95 latency under 200ms.
h2. Risks & mitigations
* **Data migration:** writes double-written during rollout. Backfill job gated by flag. * **On-call load:** new dashboards + runbook entries land in the same PR as the feature. * **Rollback:** feature flag flip restores old path within seconds.
*Estimated effort:* 5 story points. Dependencies: platform team's IAM work.

item.title: [OPS-64] Fix slow queries on indexed columns affecting admin
item.summary: Fix slow queries on indexed columns affecting admin
item.description:
h2. Context
We noticed race condition in worker pool in the websocket layer affecting admin across the eu-west-1 environment. Impact started around 2026-03-23, correlating with the v5.0-beta release. At peak we observed roughly 0.5% error rate on the affected endpoints, with users reporting slow response times and occasional 5xx errors. Support ticket volume doubled during the incident window.
h2. Reproduction steps
# Open the websocket layer in a clean session on eu-west-1 # Trigger the flow that calls POST /api/v1/webhooks/dispatch # Apply moderate load (~2500 req/s sustained for 5 minutes) # Observe latency spike and intermittent race condition in worker pool
h2. Acceptance criteria
* race condition in worker pool reproduced locally with a deterministic test harness * Root cause identified and captured in a short RFC * Regression test added that fails on main before the fix and passes after * Dashboards in Grafana updated so we'd detect this before customers do * Runbook entry in websocket layer runbook references this ticket
h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at a third-party SDK upgrade that altered retry semantics. We should check the latest changes in websocket layer that touched the POST /api/v1/webhooks/dispatch handler. Jakub Wiśniewski already pulled flamegraphs from eu-west-1; they're attached in the incident channel.
h2. Links
* Incident channel: #inc-ops-64 * Related dashboards: Grafana > websocket layer > Overview * Previous similar issue: see linked tickets
*Estimated effort:* 8 story points. Discussed in the 2026-03-23 sync. Owner: Jakub Wiśniewski.

comments.comment: Bumping priority — customer escalation came in this morning.

# What's the smallest viable change that would reduce memory leak under load by at least 50%? # Can we reuse something from the search pipeline or do we need new infra? # What's the operational cost (on-call, observability, cost per request)? # Are there open-source projects we should evaluate before building?

|| Option || Pros || Cons || Est. effort || | Build in-house | full control, fits our stack | maintenance cost, longer ship time | 8 pts | | Use OSS (Loki) | faster to prototype, active community | tuning required, unclear SLAs | 3 pts | | SaaS (Cloudflare Workers) | least upfront effort | vendor lock-in, PII and compliance | 3 pts | | Do nothing | zero cost | pain persists, risk escalates | n/a |
h2. Validation plan
* Prototype the top option in a throwaway branch * Run load test at 1000 req/s for 30 minutes on staging * Collect p50/p95/p99 latency and memory + CPU usage * Share findings in the 2026-04-02 architecture review
h2. Output
* Short ADR (architecture decision record) in `/docs/adr/` * Follow-up tickets with sized estimates * If we decide to build, a tracking Epic
*Time-box:* 3 working days. Owner: Bartek Nowak.

item.title: [PROJ-71] [Bug] Allow end user to rotate API keys without downtime
item.summary: [Bug] Allow end user to rotate API keys without downtime
item.description:
h2. Context
We noticed duplicate webhook deliveries in the embedding cache affecting developer across the eu-west-1 environment. Impact started around 2026-04-02, correlating with the v5.0-beta release. At peak we observed roughly 0.5% error rate on the affected endpoints, with users reporting slow response times and occasional 5xx errors. Support ticket volume doubled during the incident window.
h2. Reproduction steps
# Open the embedding cache in a clean session on eu-west-1 # Trigger the flow that calls PATCH /api/v2/billing/subscription # Apply moderate load (~2500 req/s sustained for 5 minutes) # Observe latency spike and intermittent duplicate webhook deliveries
h2. Acceptance criteria
* duplicate webhook deliveries reproduced locally with a deterministic test harness * Root cause identified and captured in a short RFC * Regression test added that fails on main before the fix and passes after * Dashboards in Grafana updated so we'd detect this before customers do * Runbook entry in embedding cache runbook references this ticket
h2. Technical notes
Initial investigation points at a third-party SDK upgrade that altered retry semantics. We should check the latest changes in embedding cache that touched the PATCH /api/v2/billing/subscription handler. Grace Liu already pulled flamegraphs from eu-west-1; they're attached in the incident channel.
h2. Links
* Incident channel: #inc-proj-71 * Related dashboards: Grafana > embedding cache > Overview * Previous similar issue: see linked tickets
*Estimated effort:* 13 story points. Discussed in the 2026-04-02 sync. Owner: Grace Liu.

comments.comment: This is blocked on the platform team finishing the new IAM roles.
Tagging Jakub Wiśniewski for review. Looks good apart from the missing test.

item.title: [PROJ-72] Allow billing manager to export reports as CSV
item.summary: Allow billing manager to export reports as CSV
item.description:
h2. Summary
Tracking implementation work for E2E test suite in the embedding cache. This is part of the broader effort to we save costs. The current implementation is a stop-gap from the v5.0-beta release and needs to be replaced before we onboard the next cohort.
h2. Plan
# Audit current behavior and write down the invariants we want to preserve # Draft a short RFC (RFC-524) covering data model, API contract, rollback path # Implement behind the `rollout_proj-72` feature flag with a kill switch # Cover new code with unit + integration tests; target ≥ 80% branch coverage # Roll out to the beta cohort (~25% of MAU) for 14 days # GA after 14 days of stability and no new P1 incidents
h2. Technical design notes
We considered three approaches:
|| Option || Pros || Cons || Decision || | Extend existing embedding cache | fastest to ship, reuses patterns | couples us to legacy code | | | Extract a dedicated service | clean seams, independent scaling | infra cost, more on-call | | | Use a SaaS primitive | least effort | vendor lock-in, PII concerns | rejected |
Default pick: **extend existing embedding cache** unless profiling on staging shows we can't hit the 2500 req/s target with p95 latency under 200ms.
h2. Risks & mitigations
* **Data migration:** writes double-written during rollout. Backfill job gated by flag. * **On-call load:** new dashboards + runbook entries land in the same PR as the feature. * **Rollback:** feature flag flip restores old path within seconds.
*Estimated effort:* 3 story points. Dependencies: platform team's IAM work.

item.title: [OPS-73] Improve sync engine: throttle outbound webhooks
item.summary: Improve sync engine: throttle outbound webhooks
item.description:
h2. Goal
Enable end user to filter results by tag, so that the team has visibility. Today this is painful because users have to work around it by chaining multiple manual steps, which creates support load and makes the product feel inconsistent.
h2. User story
> As a end user, > I want to filter results by tag, > so that the team has visibility.
h2. Acceptance criteria
* The graph indexer exposes a first-class UI for this action * Feature is gated behind a feature flag `rollout_ops-73` rolled out at 5% → 25% → 100% * Empty-state, loading, success and error paths are all covered * Copy reviewed by the product writer and available in English + Polish * Telemetry: at least one success event and one failure event tracked
h2. Out of scope
* Mobile parity (tracked separately) * Bulk operations on more than 100 items * Admin-level permissions for this action
h2. Open questions
# Do we need an audit trail entry for every use of this action? # Should failure notifications go to the user, the admin, or both? # What's the expected rate limit? Current proposal: 250 per user per minute.
h2. Design & references
* Figma: _graph indexer v2 → PII redaction_ frame * Linked OKR: _Reduce time-to-value for new accounts_ * Related RFC: RFC-759 (graph indexer PII redaction)
*Estimated effort:* 3 story points. Target sprint: the one after kickoff.

comments.comment: Closing as duplicate of related ticket — same root cause.
Discussed offline. We'll defer this until after the migration.
Reproduced locally on commit abc123. Working on a fix in branch fix/ops-73.
